94: // ─── Create Per-Project Relay ────────────────────────────────────────────────

96: /**
97:  * Create a per-project relay that attaches to an existing HTTP server.
98:  *
99:  * Sets up all relay components (OpenCode client, SSE consumer, translator,
100:  * session manager, bridges, WebSocket handler) and wires the full event
101:  * pipeline. Does NOT create or manage an HTTP server — the caller owns it.
102:  *
103:  * Used by both `createRelayStack()` (for standalone/skeleton mode) and the
104:  * daemon (which has its own HTTP server).
105:  */
106: export async function createProjectRelay(
107: 	config: ProjectRelayConfig,
108: ): Promise<ProjectRelay> {
109: 	const log = config.log ?? console.log;
110: 
111: 	// ── Components ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
112: 
113: 	const client = new OpenCodeClient({

121: 
122: 	// ── Per-session event cache (identical to claude-relay's doSendAndRecord) ──
123: 	const cacheDir = join(
124: 		config.projectDir ?? process.cwd(),
125: 		".opencode-relay",

127: 	);
128: 	const messageCache = new MessageCache(cacheDir);

133: 	// Per-client sliding-window rate limiter for chat messages
134: 	const rateLimiter = new RateLimiter();

136: 	// Per-session overrides (agent, model, processing timeout)
137: 	const overrides = new SessionOverrides();

139: 	// ── PTY sessions with server-side scrollback (claude-relay architecture) ──
140: 	// Each active PTY gets one upstream WebSocket to OpenCode's /pty/:id/connect.
141: 	// Output is buffered server-side (50 KB FIFO per terminal) and broadcast to
142: 	// ALL browser clients. New clients get scrollback replayed on connect.
143: 	// Input from any browser client is forwarded to the shared upstream WS.
144: 	// PTYs persist across browser show/hide toggles — only closed on explicit

154: 		//            initial shell prompt is delivered).
155: 		// cursor=-1: skip buffered data (use when reconnecting to an existing PTY
156: 		//            to avoid replaying stale escape sequences).

158: 			`/pty/${ptyId}/connect?cursor=${cursor}`,
159: 			config.opencodeUrl,
160: 		);
161: 		const wsUrl = httpUrl.toString().replace(/^http/, "ws");
162: 		const headers = client.getAuthHeaders();
163: 
164: 		return new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
165: 			const upstream = new WebSocketClass(wsUrl, {
166: 				headers,
167: 				// Disable per-message compression for PTY connections.
168: 				// Bun's WS may negotiate permessage-deflate with Node's ws, and
169: 				// decompression bugs or flush mismatches can corrupt binary PTY data.
170: 				perMessageDeflate: false,
171: 				// Skip UTF-8 validation for TEXT frames. Terminal data can contain
172: 				// 8-bit characters (C1 control codes) that are valid in terminal
173: 				// protocols but technically invalid in strict UTF-8. Without this,
174: 				// the ws library would close the connection on such frames.
175: 				skipUTF8Validation: true,
176: 			});

178: 			// Register session BEFORE any events can fire. This mirrors the
179: 			// original pattern where the PtySession object was created before
180: 			// the WebSocket — prevents the race condition where OpenCode sends

196: 				let buf: Buffer;
197: 				if (Buffer.isBuffer(data)) {
198: 					buf = data;
199: 				} else if (typeof data === "string") {
200: 					buf = Buffer.from(data);
201: 				} else {
202: 					buf = Buffer.from(data as ArrayBuffer);
203: 				}

205: 				if (buf.length > 0 && buf[0] === 0x00) return;
206: 				const text = buf.toString();
207: 				if (!text) return;

209: 				// Buffer scrollback via PtyManager (FIFO, 50 KB cap)
210: 				ptyManager.appendScrollback(ptyId, text);

222: 				if (ptyManager.hasSession(ptyId)) {

225: 						ptyId,

227: 					});

229: 				log(`   [pty] Upstream closed: ${ptyId}`);
230: 			});

232: 			upstream.on("error", (err: Error) => {
233: 				if (!connected) {
234: 					clearTimeout(connectTimeout);

236: 					ptyManager.closeSession(ptyId);

240: 			});

444: 		"unref" in timeoutTimer
445: 	) {
446: 		timeoutTimer.unref();
447: 	}
448: 
449: 	// Periodic cleanup of stale rate-limiter entries (every 60s)
450: 	const rateLimitCleanupTimer = setInterval(() => {
451: 		rateLimiter.cleanup();
452: 	}, 60_000);
453: 
454: 	if (
455: 		rateLimitCleanupTimer &&
456: 		typeof rateLimitCleanupTimer === "object" &&
457: 		"unref" in rateLimitCleanupTimer
458: 	) {
459: 		rateLimitCleanupTimer.unref();
460: 	}
461: 
462: 	// ── Return project relay ────────────────────────────────────────────────
463: 
464: 	return {
465: 		wsHandler,
466: 		sseConsumer,
467: 		client,
468: 		sessionMgr,
469: 		translator,
470: 		permissionBridge,
471: 		questionBridge,
472: 		messageCache,
473: 
474: 		async stop() {
475: 			clearInterval(timeoutTimer);
476: 			clearInterval(rateLimitCleanupTimer);
477: 			overrides.dispose();
478: 			ptyManager.closeAll();
479: 			await sseConsumer.disconnect();
480: 			wsHandler.close();
481: 		},
482: 	};
483: }
484: 
485: // ─── Create Full Stack (Server + Relay) ─────────────────────────────────────
486: 
487: /**
488:  * Create a full relay stack with its own HTTP server.
489:  *
490:  * Creates a RelayServer, registers the project, starts the server, then
491:  * delegates to `createProjectRelay()` for all relay wiring. Used by
492:  * skeleton.ts for standalone operation.
493:  */
494: export async function createRelayStack(
495: 	config: RelayStackConfig,
496: ): Promise<RelayStack> {
497: 	const log = config.log ?? console.log;
498: 
499: 	// ── Push notification manager ────────────────────────────────────────────
500: 
501: 	let pushMgr: PushNotificationManager | undefined = config.pushManager;
502: 	if (!pushMgr) {
503: 		try {
504: 			const { PushNotificationManager } = await import("./push.js");
505: 			pushMgr = new PushNotificationManager();
506: 			await pushMgr.init();
507: 		} catch {
508: 			pushMgr = undefined;
509: 		}
510: 	}
511: 
512: 	// ── HTTP server ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
513: 
514: 	const server = new RelayServer({
515: 		port: config.port,
516: 		host: config.host,
517: 		pin: config.pin || undefined,
518: 		staticDir: config.staticDir,
519: 		tls: config.tls,
520: 		pushManager: pushMgr,
521: 	});
522: 
523: 	server.addProject({
524: 		slug: config.slug,
525: 		directory: config.projectDir,
526: 		title: config.slug,
527: 	});
528: 
529: 	await server.start();
530: 
531: 	const maybeServer = server.getHttpServer();
532: 	if (!maybeServer) {
533: 		throw new Error("HTTP server not available after start()");
534: 	}
535: 	// Assign to a fresh const so TypeScript narrows to non-null in closures.
536: 	const httpServer = maybeServer;
537: 
538: 	// ── Multi-project relay management ──────────────────────────────────────
539: 	// All relays use noServer mode. A single upgrade handler routes WebSocket
540: 	// connections to the correct relay by URL path (/ws → initial, /p/{slug}/ws → project).
541: 	// This matches the daemon pattern and allows dynamic project addition.
542: 
543: 	const relays = new Map<string, ProjectRelay>();
544: 	const pendingSlugs = new Set<string>();
545: 
546: 	const getProjectList = () =>
547: 		server.getProjects().map((p) => ({
548: 			slug: p.slug,
549: 			title: p.title,
550: 			directory: p.directory,
551: 		}));
552: 
553: 	/** Create a new project relay and register it. */
554: 	async function addProjectRelay(
555: 		directory: string,
556: 	): Promise<{ slug: string; title: string; directory: string }> {
557: 		// Expand ~ and resolve to absolute path
558: 		if (directory.startsWith("~/") || directory === "~") {
559: 			directory = directory.replace("~", homedir());
560: 		}
561: 		directory = resolve(directory);
562: 
563: 		// Check if directory is already registered
564: 		for (const p of server.getProjects()) {
565: 			if (p.directory === directory) {
566: 				return { slug: p.slug, title: p.title, directory: p.directory };
567: 			}
568: 		}
569: 
570: 		// Validate directory exists on disk
571: 		const dirStat = await stat(directory).catch(() => null);
572: 		if (!dirStat?.isDirectory()) {
573: 			throw new Error(
574: 				dirStat
575: 					? `Not a directory: ${directory}`
576: 					: `Directory does not exist: ${directory}`,
577: 			);
578: 		}
579: 
580: 		const existingSlugs = new Set(relays.keys());
581: 		const slug = generateSlug(directory, existingSlugs);
582: 		const parts = directory.replace(/\\/g, "/").split("/").filter(Boolean);
583: 		const title = parts[parts.length - 1] ?? "project";
584: 
585: 		// Guard against concurrent creation for the same slug
586: 		if (relays.has(slug) || pendingSlugs.has(slug)) {
587: 			const existing = relays.get(slug);
588: 			if (existing) return { slug, title, directory };
589: 			throw new Error(`Relay for ${directory} is still being created`);
590: 		}
591: 
592: 		pendingSlugs.add(slug);
593: 		try {
594: 			// Create relay FIRST — if this throws, nothing is registered
595: 			const newRelay = await createProjectRelay({
596: 				httpServer,
597: 				opencodeUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
598: 				projectDir: directory,
599: 				slug,
600: 				noServer: true,
601: 				sessionTitle: config.sessionTitle,
602: 				log,
603: 				getProjects: getProjectList,
604: 				addProject: addProjectRelay,
605: 				pushManager: pushMgr,
606: 			});
607: 
608: 			// Only register AFTER relay is successfully created
609: 			relays.set(slug, newRelay);
610: 			server.addProject({ slug, directory, title });
611: 
612: 			log(`   [project] Added: ${title} (${slug}) → ${directory}`);
613: 		} catch (err) {
614: 			// Clean up on failure — no zombie entries
615: 			relays.delete(slug);
616: 			log(`   [project] Failed to add ${directory}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`);
617: 			throw err;
618: 		} finally {
619: 			pendingSlugs.delete(slug);
620: 		}
621: 
622: 		return { slug, title, directory };
623: 	}
624: 
625: 	// ── Initial project relay ───────────────────────────────────────────────
626: 
627: 	const relay = await createProjectRelay({
628: 		httpServer,
629: 		opencodeUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
630: 		projectDir: config.projectDir,
631: 		slug: config.slug,
632: 		sessionTitle: config.sessionTitle,
633: 		log,
634: 		noServer: true,
635: 		getProjects: getProjectList,
636: 		addProject: addProjectRelay,
637: 		pushManager: pushMgr,
638: 	});
639: 	relays.set(config.slug, relay);
640: 
641: 	// ── WebSocket upgrade handler ───────────────────────────────────────────
642: 	// Routes connections by URL: /p/{slug}/ws → project relay, /ws → initial relay.
643: 	// Also checks auth when a PIN is configured (fixes pre-existing gap where
644: 	// standalone WS connections bypassed PIN auth).
645: 
646: 	httpServer.on("upgrade", (req, socket, head) => {
647: 		// Auth check (mirrors server.ts private checkAuth)
648: 		const auth = server.getAuth();
649: 		if (auth.hasPin()) {
650: 			const cookies = parseCookies(req.headers.cookie ?? "");
651: 			const sessionCookie = cookies.relay_session;
652: 			const cookieOk = sessionCookie
653: 				? auth.validateCookie(sessionCookie)
654: 				: false;
655: 			if (!cookieOk) {
656: 				const pinHeader = req.headers["x-relay-pin"];
657: 				const pinOk =
658: 					typeof pinHeader === "string" &&
659: 					auth.authenticate(pinHeader, getClientIp(req)).ok;
660: 				if (!pinOk) {
661: 					socket.destroy();
662: 					return;
663: 				}
664: 			}
665: 		}
666: 
667: 		// Route /p/{slug}/ws → project relay
668: 		const projectMatch = req.url?.match(/^\/p\/([^/]+)\/ws(?:\?|$)/);
669: 		if (projectMatch) {
670: 			const target = relays.get(projectMatch[1]!);
671: 			if (target) {
672: 				target.wsHandler.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head);
673: 			} else {
674: 				socket.destroy();
675: 			}
676: 			return;
677: 		}
678: 
679: 		// Route /ws → initial relay
680: 		if (req.url === "/ws" || req.url?.startsWith("/ws?")) {
681: 			relay.wsHandler.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head);
682: 			return;
683: 		}
684: 
685: 		socket.destroy();
686: 	});
687: 
688: 	const urls = server.getUrls();
689: 	log(`   ✓ Server listening: ${urls.local}`);
690: 
691: 	return {
692: 		server,
693: 		wsHandler: relay.wsHandler,
694: 		sseConsumer: relay.sseConsumer,
695: 		client: relay.client,
696: 		sessionMgr: relay.sessionMgr,
697: 		translator: relay.translator,
698: 		permissionBridge: relay.permissionBridge,
699: 		questionBridge: relay.questionBridge,
700: 
701: 		getPort() {
702: 			const addr = httpServer.address();
703: 			if (typeof addr === "object" && addr) return addr.port;
704: 			return config.port;
705: 		},
706: 
707: 		getBaseUrl() {
708: 			const addr = httpServer.address();
709: 			const port = typeof addr === "object" && addr ? addr.port : config.port;
710: 			const protocol = config.tls ? "https" : "http";
711: 			return `${protocol}://127.0.0.1:${port}`;
712: 		},
713: 
714: 		async stop() {
715: 			for (const r of relays.values()) {
716: 				try {
717: 					await r.stop();
718: 				} catch {
719: 					/* ignore */
720: 				}
721: 			}
722: 			relays.clear();
723: 			await server.stop();
724: 		},
725: 	};
726: }

<content>1: // ─── Session Management Layer (Ticket 2.3) ───────────────────────────────────
2: // Manages the mapping between OpenCode sessions and the relay's representation.
3: // OpenCode (SQLite) is always the source of truth — the relay never duplicates
4: // storage. This layer proxies session CRUD and maintains in-memory active state.
5: 
6: import { EventEmitter } from "node:events";
7: import type {
8: 	Message,
9: 	OpenCodeClient,
10: 	SessionDetail,
11: } from "./opencode-client.js";
12: import type { RelayMessage, SessionInfo } from "./types.js";
13: 
14: // ─── Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
15: 
16: export interface SessionManagerOptions {
17: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
18: 	/** Number of messages to load per page (default 50) */
19: 	historyPageSize?: number;
20: }
21: 
22: export interface SessionManagerEvents {
23: 	/** Broadcast this message to all connected clients */
24: 	broadcast: [RelayMessage];
25: 	/** Send to a specific client */
26: 	send: [{ clientId: string; message: RelayMessage }];
27: 	/** Active session changed */
28: 	session_changed: [{ sessionId: string }];
29: }
30: 
31: export interface HistoryPage {
32: 	messages: Message[];
33: 	hasMore: boolean;
34: 	/** Total messages in the session (if known) */
35: 	total?: number;
36: }
37: 
38: // ─── Session Manager ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
39: 
40: export class SessionManager extends EventEmitter<SessionManagerEvents> {
41: 	private readonly client: OpenCodeClient;
42: 	private readonly historyPageSize: number;
43: 	private activeSessionId: string | null = null;
44: 
45: 	constructor(options: SessionManagerOptions) {
46: 		super();
47: 		this.client = options.client;
48: 		this.historyPageSize = options.historyPageSize ?? 50;
49: 	}
50: 
51: 	// ─── Queries ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
52: 
53: 	/** Get the active session ID */
54: 	getActiveSessionId(): string | null {
55: 		return this.activeSessionId;
56: 	}
57: 
58: 	/** List sessions sorted by most recently updated first */
59: 	async listSessions(): Promise<SessionInfo[]> {
60: 		const sessions = await this.client.listSessions();
61: 		return toSessionInfoList(sessions);
62: 	}
63: 
64: 	/**
65: 	 * Load a page of message history for a session.
66: 	 *
67: 	 * Messages are returned in chronological order (oldest first).
68: 	 * Pagination loads from the END (most recent) backwards:
69: 	 *   offset=0  → most recent pageSize messages
70: 	 *   offset=50 → the 50 messages before those
71: 	 *
72: 	 * This matches chat UI expectations: show newest messages first,
73: 	 * lazy-load older messages as the user scrolls up.
74: 	 */
75: 	async loadHistory(sessionId: string, offset = 0): Promise<HistoryPage> {
76: 		const all = await this.client.getMessages(sessionId);
77: 		const total = all.length;
78: 
79: 		// Slice from the end, moving backwards by `offset`
80: 		const end = Math.max(0, total - offset);
81: 		const start = Math.max(0, end - this.historyPageSize);
82: 		const page = all.slice(start, end);
83: 
84: 		return {
85: 			messages: page,
86: 			hasMore: start > 0,
87: 			total,
88: 		};
89: 	}
90: 
91: 	// ─── Mutations ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
92: 
93: 	/** Create a new session and make it active */
94: 	async createSession(title?: string): Promise<SessionDetail> {
95: 		const session = await this.client.createSession({
96: 			title: title ?? "New Session",
97: 		});
98: 		this.activeSessionId = session.id;
99: 
100: 		this.emit("session_changed", { sessionId: session.id });
101: 		this.emit("broadcast", { type: "session_switched", id: session.id });
102: 		await this.broadcastSessionList();
103: 
104: 		return session;
105: 	}
106: 
107: 	/**
108: 	 * Switch to an existing session.
109: 	 *
110: 	 * Does NOT broadcast session_switched — the caller (relay-stack.ts) handles
111: 	 * that with the combined protocol (events or REST API fallback inline).
112: 	 * Emits session_changed so translator resets and SSE filter updates.
113: 	 */
114: 	async switchSession(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
115: 		this.activeSessionId = sessionId;
116: 		this.emit("session_changed", { sessionId });
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	/** Delete a session. If it was active, switch to the most recent remaining. */
120: 	async deleteSession(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
121: 		await this.client.deleteSession(sessionId);
122: 
123: 		if (this.activeSessionId === sessionId) {
124: 			// Switch to most recent remaining session
125: 			const remaining = await this.listSessions();
126: 			if (remaining.length > 0) {
127: 				this.activeSessionId = remaining[0]!.id;
128: 				this.emit("broadcast", {
129: 					type: "session_switched",
130: 					id: this.activeSessionId,
131: 				});
132: 				this.emit("session_changed", { sessionId: this.activeSessionId });
133: 			} else {
134: 				this.activeSessionId = null;
135: 			}
136: 		}
137: 
138: 		await this.broadcastSessionList();
139: 	}
140: 
141: 	/** Rename a session */
142: 	async renameSession(sessionId: string, title: string): Promise<void> {
143: 		await this.client.updateSession(sessionId, { title });
144: 		await this.broadcastSessionList();
145: 	}
146: 
147: 	/** Search sessions by query */
148: 	async searchSessions(query: string): Promise<SessionInfo[]> {
149: 		// OpenCode supports search via query param
150: 		const sessions = await this.client.listSessions();
151: 		// Client-side filter since OpenCode's list endpoint may not support search directly
152: 		const q = query.toLowerCase();
153: 		const matches = sessions.filter((s) => {
154: 			return (
155: 				(s.title ?? "").toLowerCase().includes(q) ||
156: 				s.id.toLowerCase().includes(q)
157: 			);
158: 		});
159: 		return toSessionInfoList(matches);
160: 	}
161: 
162: 	/**
163: 	 * Initialize: always create a fresh session.
164: 	 * Previous sessions remain in the list for users to switch to, but a new
165: 	 * session ensures a clean slate (no stuck messages from prior model mismatches).
166: 	 */
167: 	async initialize(title?: string): Promise<string> {
168: 		const session = await this.client.createSession({
169: 			title: title ?? "Relay Session",
170: 		});
171: 		this.activeSessionId = session.id;
172: 		return this.activeSessionId;
173: 	}
174: 
175: 	/** Set active session directly (e.g., for SSE filter coordination) */
176: 	setActiveSessionId(sessionId: string): void {
177: 		this.activeSessionId = sessionId;
178: 	}
179: 
180: 	// ─── Internal ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
181: 
182: 	private async broadcastSessionList(): Promise<void> {
183: 		const sessions = await this.listSessions();
184: 		this.emit("broadcast", { type: "session_list", sessions });
185: 	}
186: }
187: 
188: // ─── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
189: 
190: /** Convert OpenCode SessionDetail[] → sorted SessionInfo[] for the frontend */
191: function toSessionInfoList(sessions: SessionDetail[]): SessionInfo[] {
192: 	return sessions
193: 		.map((s) => ({
194: 			id: s.id,
195: 			title: s.title ?? "Untitled",
196: 			updatedAt: s.time?.updated ?? s.time?.created ?? 0,
197: 			messageCount: 0, // OpenCode doesn't include this in list; frontend can fetch if needed
198: 		}))
199: 		.sort((a, b) => b.updatedAt - a.updatedAt);
200: }

2: // Starts the real relay stack pointed at a real OpenCode instance.
3: // E2E tests connect via Playwright browser to the relay's HTTP server.
4: //
5: // Set env vars to control model (defaults to free Gemini Flash):
6: //   E2E_MODEL=gemini-2.0-flash  E2E_PROVIDER=google
7: 
8: import path from "node:path";
9: import WebSocket from "ws";
10: import {
11: 	createRelayStack,
12: 	type RelayStack,
13: } from "../../../src/lib/relay-stack.js";
14: 
15: const OPENCODE_URL = process.env.OPENCODE_URL ?? "http://localhost:4096";
16: // Only switch model if BOTH env vars are explicitly set — otherwise use OpenCode's default
17: const E2E_MODEL = process.env.E2E_MODEL ?? "";
18: const E2E_PROVIDER = process.env.E2E_PROVIDER ?? "";
19: 
20: export interface E2EHarness {
21: 	stack: RelayStack;
22: 	opencodeUrl: string;
23: 	relayPort: number;
24: 	relayBaseUrl: string;
25: 	model: string;
26: 	provider: string;
27: 	stop(): Promise<void>;
28: }
29: 
30: /** Check if OpenCode is reachable */
31: export async function isOpenCodeRunning(url?: string): Promise<boolean> {
32: 	try {
33: 		const res = await fetch(`${url ?? OPENCODE_URL}/path`, {
34: 			signal: AbortSignal.timeout(3000),
35: 		});
36: 		return res.ok;
37: 	} catch {
38: 		return false;
39: 	}
40: }
41: 
42: /**
43:  * Send a switch_model message to the relay via a temporary WS connection.
44:  * This sets the model for all subsequent prompts from any browser client.
45:  */
46: async function switchToFreeModel(relayPort: number): Promise<void> {
47: 	if (!E2E_MODEL || !E2E_PROVIDER) return;
48: 
49: 	return new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
50: 		const ws = new WebSocket(`ws://127.0.0.1:${relayPort}/ws`);
51: 		const timer = setTimeout(() => {
52: 			ws.close();
53: 			reject(new Error("Timeout switching model"));
54: 		}, 5000);
55: 
56: 		ws.on("open", () => {
57: 			ws.send(
58: 				JSON.stringify({
59: 					type: "switch_model",
60: 					modelId: E2E_MODEL,
61: 					providerId: E2E_PROVIDER,
62: 				}),
63: 			);
64: 			// Give the relay a moment to process, then close
65: 			setTimeout(() => {
66: 				clearTimeout(timer);
67: 				ws.close();
68: 				resolve();
69: 			}, 300);
70: 		});
71: 
72: 		ws.on("error", (err) => {
73: 			clearTimeout(timer);
74: 			reject(err);
75: 		});
76: 	});
77: }
78: 
79: /** Create a relay pointed at real OpenCode, serving the built frontend */
80: export async function createE2EHarness(opts?: {
81: 	opencodeUrl?: string;
82: }): Promise<E2EHarness> {
83: 	const opencodeUrl = opts?.opencodeUrl ?? OPENCODE_URL;
84: 
85: 	// dist/public/ is relative to the project root (opencode-relay/)
86: 	const staticDir = path.resolve(import.meta.dirname, "../../../dist/public");
87: 
88: 	const stack = await createRelayStack({
89: 		port: 0,
90: 		host: "127.0.0.1",
91: 		opencodeUrl,
92: 		projectDir: process.cwd(),
93: 		slug: "e2e-test",
94: 		sessionTitle: "E2E Test Session",
95: 		staticDir,
96: 		log: () => {}, // Silence relay logs during tests
97: 	});
98: 
99: 	const relayPort = stack.getPort();
100: 	const relayBaseUrl = `http://127.0.0.1:${relayPort}`;
101: 
102: 	// Switch to free-tier model for cost-free E2E testing
103: 	await switchToFreeModel(relayPort);
104: 
105: 	return {
106: 		stack,
107: 		opencodeUrl,
108: 		relayPort,
109: 		relayBaseUrl,
110: 		model: E2E_MODEL,
111: 		provider: E2E_PROVIDER,
112: 		async stop(): Promise<void> {
113: 			await stack.stop();
114: 		},
115: 	};
116: }
